calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Freedman for Niall MacLennan, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The Court addressed the probate examiner's notes regarding the issue of bond waiver. Mr. Freedman stated he did not object to a bond being set and clarified the unique circumstances of the matter, including the lack of a professional fiduciary willing to serve and the small size of the estate.

After discussion, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration as follows: Michael Freedman is appointed Administrator. Bond is set in the amount of $58,000. Amy Harrington is appointed Probate Referee. Status on close of estate is set for July 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If a petition for final distribution or a Probate Code Sec. 12200 petition is filed at least two weeks prior, the status hearing will go off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Michael Freedman via zoom for Niall Maclennan (petitioner).

Mr. Freedman stated that he will file an amended petition. The matter is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Continued To Apr-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Freedman for Niall MacLennan, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The Court inquired about the status of the petition. Mr. Friedman reported that the key issue involves determining the heirs at law, which has now been largely resolved. However, the appointment of a personal representative for a deceased heir, Brian Kelly, is still pending in Scotland. He explained that initial confusion regarding heirship terminology among the heirs in Scotland has since been clarified. Mr. Friedman confirmed that he will act as administrator, given the lack of any other U.S.-based contacts willing to do so. Mr. Friedman requested 45 to 60 days to allow time for the appointment process in Scotland to finalize and indicated that an amended petition would be filed once the appointment is confirmed.

The court continued the matter to April 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
